notes: Route codes extracted from Fillone et al. service contracting AVL study (2022). These are jeepney routes under COVID-era service contracting (Nov 2020 – Jun 2021). Origin/destination pairs and stop data NOT published; only route codes and operator names available. T-prefix routes are PUVMP-consolidated modern jeepney routes; numeric-only codes (201, 302, 305, etc.) may be pre-consolidation or traditional route codes.

routes.frequency_notes: Service contracting period Nov 2020–Jun 2021; gross-cost scheme resulted in shorter headways than net-cost
routes.notes: Operator: PM Jeepney Drivers and Operators Services Inc. Route studied via smartphone AVL app (Sakay Mobility Philippines Corp). No published O/D or geometry. Source: Fillone et al. 2022 PMC8828415.
Operator: Saint Rose Transit. Route studied via smartphone AVL app. No published O/D or geometry. Source: Fillone et al. 2022 PMC8828415.

routes.frequency_notes: Service contracting period Nov 2020–Jun 2021; T-prefix = PUVMP consolidated modern jeepney
routes.notes: Operator: San Dionisio Transport Service Cooperative. T-prefix = modern PUJ (PUVMP consolidated route). No published O/D or geometry. Source: Fillone et al. 2022 PMC8828415.
Operator: A. Roces Transport Service Cooperative. No published O/D or geometry. Source: Fillone et al. 2022 PMC8828415.
Operator: Taguig Transport Service Cooperative. Route 201 prefix suggests Taguig area. No published O/D or geometry. Source: Fillone et al. 2022 PMC8828415.
Operator: Malabon Jeepney Transport Service Cooperative (MAJETSCO). Route 305 prefix suggests Malabon/Caloocan area. No published O/D or geometry. Source: Fillone et al. 2022 PMC8828415.
